report_name: AI-Powered Ransomware 'JadePuffer' Deploys 'ENCFORGE' to Destroy ML Models

threat_actors.name: JadePuffer
threat_actors.description: An autonomous, AI-driven threat actor that uses a Large Language Model (LLM) to conduct end-to-end attacks. In this campaign, it deployed the ENCFORGE ransomware to destroy AI/ML models after exploiting a vulnerability in Langflow to escape a container.
threat_actors.goals: Targeted destruction of AI and machine learning intellectual property.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The JadePuffer agent exploited CVE-2025-3248, a missing authentication vulnerability in the public-facing Langflow MLOps platform, to gain initial code execution within a container.

attack_patterns.name: Escape to Host
attack_patterns.description: The threat agent leveraged a mounted Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) from within a compromised container to spawn a new, privileged container on the host system.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: The ENCFORGE payload was executed on the host to find and encrypt AI/ML model files, with the primary goal of destroying intellectual property rather than extortion.

attack_patterns.name: File Deletion
attack_patterns.description: The ENCFORGE malware self-deletes upon completion of its destructive routine to hinder forensic analysis.

malware.name: ENCFORGE
malware.description: A purpose-built destructive payload written in Go and packed with UPX. It is designed to find and encrypt files associated with the AI/ML development lifecycle, effectively wiping them. It targets over 180 file extensions, kills processes with file locks, and self-deletes after execution.

tools.name: UPX
tools.description: Used to pack the ENCFORGE ransomware payload.

tools.name: Docker
tools.description: The Docker socket mounted in the container was abused by the JadePuffer agent to spawn a new privileged container on the host, enabling the container escape.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2025-3248
vulnerabilities.description: A missing authentication vulnerability in Langflow's code validation endpoint, which was exploited by JadePuffer to gain initial code execution within a container.

identities.name: AI-developing organizations
identities.description: A class of organizations focused on developing AI and machine learning models, targeted by the JadePuffer threat actor for intellectual property destruction.

indicators.name: ENCFORGE File Artifact
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: ENCFORGE Destructive Malware Execution
id: 2b1f8c7d-9e3a-4f1b-8c7d-9e3a4f1b8c7d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the ENCFORGE destructive malware binary.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/jadepuffer-ai-model-ransomware/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1485
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/encforge'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the ENCFORGE malware, which is used to destroy AI/ML models.

indicators.name: Suspicious Langflow Code Validation Endpoint Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Langflow Code Validation Endpoint Access
id: 8a3c1e9b-5d2f-4b1a-9c8d-7e6f5d4c3b2a
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious or repeated requests to the vulnerable Langflow /api/v1/validate_code endpoint, which could indicate an exploitation attempt for CVE-2025-3248.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/jadepuffer-ai-model-ransomware/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: high
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    cs-uri-stem|contains: '/api/v1/validate_code'
    cs-method: 'POST'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects access to the vulnerable Langflow endpoint `/api/v1/validate_code` which was used for initial access.

indicators.name: Privileged Docker Container Spawn from Container
indicators.pattern:
title: Privileged Docker Container Spawn from Container
id: 1f9b8d7c-3a2e-4f1b-8c7d-6e5f4d3c2b1a
status: experimental
description: Detects a command originating from within a container that attempts to spawn a new privileged container via a mounted Docker socket, a technique used for container escape.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/jadepuffer-ai-model-ransomware/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1611
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/docker'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'run'
      - '--privileged'
  filter_known_good_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '/sshd'
      - '/bash'
      - '/zsh'
  condition: selection and not filter_known_good_parent
indicators.description: Detects the `docker run --privileged` command used to escape the container.

indicators.name: Anomalous Modification of ML Model Files
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Modification of ML Model Files
id: 5e4d3c2b-1a9f-4b8d-7c6e-5f4d3c2b1a9f
status: experimental
description: Detects high-volume read or write activity on files with common ML model extensions, which could indicate a destructive attack like ENCFORGE.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/jadepuffer-ai-model-ransomware/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1485
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.pt'
      - '.pth'
      - '.ckpt'
      - '.safetensors'
      - '.gguf'
      - '.faiss'
      - '.parquet'
      - '.tfrecord'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects high-volume read/write activity on files with ML model extensions, a key behavior of ENCFORGE.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Langflow Code Validation Endpoint Access
Privileged Docker Container Spawn from Container
Anomalous Modification of ML Model Files
